title: Garen Muln
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Character |type=Jedi |image= |name=Garen Muln |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[18 BBY/Legends|18 BBY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' [[Jedi asteroid]]''[[The Last of the Jedi: Reckoning]]'' |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]''[[Jedi Apprentice: The Rising Force]]'' |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] *[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] *[[Separatist holdouts/Legends|Separatist holdouts]]''[[The Last of the Jedi: Secret Weapon]]'' |masters=[[Clee Rhara]]''[[Jedi Apprentice: The Death of Hope]]'' |apprentices=[[Lune Oddo Divinian]] }} {{Quote|As Garen would say, piece of sweetcake.|Siri Tachi|''[[Jedi Quest: The Changing of the Guard]]''}} '''Garen Muln''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] who served the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] as a [[Jedi General/Legends|General]] in the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. ==Biography== ===Early life=== A [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Human/Legends|Human]], Garen Muln was brought to the [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] at an early age where he entered a [[Clan (Jedi)/Legends|clan]] of other [[Jedi Initiate/Legends|Initiates]] and was taught the ways of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. A restless adolescent, Muln was a contemporary of [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and maintained a good friendship despite arguing over trivial things. When a [[Xanatos|dark sider]] [[Incident at the Jedi Temple|infiltrated]] the Temple in [[44 BBY/Legends|44 BBY]], Initiate Muln worked to thwart the intruder by disguising himself as Padawan Kenobi to help throw off the intruder's plan. While Muln and Master [[Ali-Alann]] posed as Kenobi and Master [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]], the pair were able to capture the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] and protect the Temple from destruction.''[[Jedi Apprentice: The Captive Temple]]'' Selected by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Clee Rhara]] as a [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] circa [[42 BBY/Legends|42 BBY]], the two continued to train as a Jedi as they traveled across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Eventually passing his [[Jedi Trials/Legends|Trials of Knighthood]], Jedi Muln went onto train as a [[Jedi ace|ace]] in the [[Jedi Starfighter Corps]], aiding his former master establish a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] base on [[Centax-2|Centax 2]] during the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]] which plagued the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]].''[[Jedi Apprentice Special Edition: Deceptions]]'' In the following years, Jedi Muln took on several missions, pairing with his childhood acquaintance Jedi [[Siri Tachi/Legends|Siri Tachi]] on a rescue mission to save Padawan [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] from the clutches of the deranged [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] [[Jenna Zan Arbor]]'s laboratory on [[Vanqor/Legends|Vanqor]].''[[Jedi Quest: The Moment of Truth]]'' Soon after, Muln accompanied [[Master of the Order/Legends|Master of the Order]] [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] and his long time friend [[Bant Eerin/Legends|Bant Eerin]] to the planet [[Romin]] where they rescued Jedi Kenobi, Tachi and Padawans Skywalker and [[Ferus Olin]], once more from death at the hands of Zan Arbor and the dangerous [[Granta Omega]]. ===Clone Wars=== When the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] erupted across the galaxy between the Republic and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], Jedi Muln took up the rank of [[Jedi General/Legends|General]] within the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]], adding his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] to the war effort against the [[Separatist Droid Army/Legends|Separatist Droid Army]]. Obtaining the rank of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] by the time of the final year of the war, Master Muln was assigned to lead a small group of [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] against the Confederacy occupation on the world of [[Acherin]]. While there, Master Muln negotiated with Confederate commanders [[Toma (resistance leader)|Toma]] and [[Raina Quill]] in hopes of reaching a truce.''[[The Last of the Jedi: Dark Warning]]'' As the negotiators began to reach an agreement, [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] of the Republic betrayed the Jedi Order and issued [[Contingency Orders for the Grand Army of the Republic: Order Initiation, Orders 1 Through 150|Contingency]] [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]] to the Grand Army, branding the Order as traitors. Able to escape his clone troopers' wrath, Muln escaped the world with the help of Commanders Toma and Quill, his former opponents. ===Exile=== {{Quote|Can I get you anything?''"
"''Just don't bring me a mirror. I can see on your face how bad it is.|Obi-Wan Kenobi and Garen Muln en route to a hidden asteroid|}} Having fled Acherin, an injured and weary Master Muln piloted his craft to the Jedi fortress world of [[Ilum/Legends|Ilum]] and take refuge from the frozen wastes in the [[Crystal Cave/Legends|Temple there]]. Ensconced within the frozen temple, Master Muln met another Jedi, [[Jedi instructor|Temple instructor]] [[Fy-Tor-Ana]]. Master Ana detailed the destruction of the Temple on Coruscant and the death of the Order before she left Ilum to go seek out survivors of the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]]. Remaining in the Temple's caves for nearly a year, Muln was weakened from malnutrition by the time former Padawan [[Ferus Olin]] arrived in search of him. Informing Master Muln that his old friend, Obi-Wan Kenobi was alive and in exile also, Olin managed to convince Muln to leave the world and embark on another mission. Reuniting with Master Kenobi in orbit, Muln was too weak to talk with his friend during the journey to Olin's [[Jedi asteroid|secret asteroid]]. Olin planned on converting the asteroid's barren surface into a makeshift sanctuary for fugitive Jedi, starting with Muln. Once safe in the asteroid's interior, Master Muln was healed by an old acquaintance, Commander Quill, who was also with Toma. Recovering slowly, Master Muln found himself useful once more when he took over the training of the young [[Lune Oddo Divinian]], a Force-sensitive boy whose training was started by Master [[Ry-Gaul]]. Taking over the training, Muln instilled in Divinian the virtues of the Jedi Path while remaining on the asteroid. The solace that the group of exiles had found on the asteroid would be shattered too soon when [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] and servant of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] located the base and attacked. Led there by information leaked by General Toma and a spy, [[Eve Yarrow]], whom the group had trusted, the Jedi were stranded with nothing more than an [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pod]]. Master Muln, joined by Masters Ana and Ry-Gaul, loaded Lune Divinian into the pod and managed to get him off world before the attack could claim his life. Accepting that the hope of the Jedi would lay on the shoulders of younglings like Divinian, the three Masters accepted their fates as Jedi when Vader fired upon the asteroid with his [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]], killing them. ==Appearances== {{ScrollBox|content= *''[[Jedi Apprentice: The Rising Force]]'' {{1st}} *''[[Jedi Apprentice: The Captive Temple]]'' *''[[Jedi Apprentice: The Day of Reckoning]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[Jedi Apprentice Special Edition: Deceptions]]'' *''[[Jedi Apprentice: The Evil Experiment]]'' {{Mo}} *[[Secrets of the Jedi (novel)|''Secrets of the Jedi'']] {{Mo}} *''[[Jedi Quest: The Moment of Truth]]'' *''[[Jedi Quest: The Changing of the Guard]]'' *''[[The Last of the Jedi: The Desperate Mission]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[The Last of the Jedi: Dark Warning]]'' *''[[The Last of the Jedi: Underworld]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[The Last of the Jedi: Death on Naboo]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[The Last of the Jedi: A Tangled Web]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[The Last of the Jedi: Return of the Dark Side]]'' *''[[The Last of the Jedi: Secret Weapon]]'' *''[[The Last of the Jedi: Against the Empire]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[The Last of the Jedi: Master of Deception]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[The Last of the Jedi: Reckoning]]'' *''[[Rebel Force: Hostage]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[Rebel Force: Firefight]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[Rebel Force: Trapped]]'' {{Mo}} }} ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Garen Muln |es=Garen Muln |pl=Garen Muln |pt=Garen Muln }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Muln, Garen}} [[Category:Confederacy military personnel]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Jedi Generals]] [[Category:Jedi Purge victims]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Post-Ruusan Jedi Masters]] [[Category:Separatist holdouts individuals]]
